description: Default medical-domain risk classifications. Override per deployment via RiskConfig.

prescribe_medication.rationale: Once a prescription is filled and dispensed, the action is irreversible. Drug interactions and dosing errors can cause harm.

discontinue_medication.rationale: Reversible (can be re-prescribed) but high-risk for conditions requiring uninterrupted therapy.

order_lab_test.rationale: Reversible (can be cancelled before draw) but has cost and patient-time implications.

order_imaging.rationale: Reversible (can be cancelled) but X-ray, CT, and PET expose patients to ionizing radiation.

send_referral.rationale: Reversible with effort (can be withdrawn) but creates appointments and patient expectations.

update_medical_record.rationale: Reversible with audit trail; record edits are tracked but not retroactively removable.

create_clinical_note.rationale: Reversible with audit; signed notes become part of the legal record.

read_patient_chart.rationale: Reversible (no state change) but generates audit log entries per HIPAA.

search_drug_database.rationale: Read-only reference lookup with no patient-identifier exposure.

lookup_icd_code.rationale: Reference lookup of diagnostic codes; no patient data accessed.

check_drug_interaction.rationale: Computed against drug database; no patient data persisted.

schedule_appointment.rationale: Reversible (can be cancelled) but creates patient and staff commitments.

cancel_appointment.rationale: Reversible (can be re-scheduled) but disrupts care continuity.
